review_scope: Phase 1 scene setup, root rig, whole-cell context socket, builder report, manifest, Roblox export rules, checklist, and review render.

export_001_transforms_are_applied_before.notes: No export step exists in Phase 1 and the script does not explicitly apply transforms before a Roblox-bound export. The visible attach orbit has rotation set procedurally, which must be applied or exported through a controlled export-only collection in a later pass.

export_002_origins_and_pivots_support_in.notes: Lysosome_RootRig and WholeCell_ContextSocket are centered at the origin and parented in a sensible hierarchy for later whole-cell attachment and movement.

export_003_triangle_and_material_risks_a.notes: The builder report lists general material and future detail risks, but it does not provide triangle count, object count, material count, texture count, or a concrete Roblox budget.

export_004_exported_component_names_are_.notes: Created major components use exact manifest names: WholeCell_ContextSocket and Lysosome_RootRig. Helper visual objects also use stable descriptive names.

export_005_missing_manifest_components_a.notes: The builder report correctly reports Lysosome_Membrane, AcidicInterior_Volume, DigestiveEnzyme_Clusters, WasteCargo_Fragments, RecycledOutput_Cues, and Interaction_Hotspots as incomplete.

major_failures: No Roblox export artifact or export manifest is produced for Phase 1, so the phase cannot be considered an export-ready package.
No explicit transform-application or export-only collection workflow is present for the generated socket geometry.
Required export review fields from ROBLOX_EXPORT_RULES.md are incomplete: exported files and manual setup notes are not listed.
Triangle count, material count, texture count, and object count are not measured or recorded.
minor_issues: Procedural Blender material nodes and the displacement texture on the visible socket core will not transfer directly to Roblox without baking or simplification.
The review render shows only a socket marker and attach orbit, which is acceptable for Phase 1 scope but provides no evidence for final lysosome collision, LOD, transparency, or animation behavior.
The visible socket core is a UV sphere placeholder; it should remain an integration marker and not become part of the final exported lysosome biology mesh unless documented as a debug-only component.
Future transparent acidic interior effects are already identified as a risk, but no Roblox approximation strategy is specified yet.
required_fixes: Add a Phase 1 export-readiness section or companion report that lists exported files, component names, missing manifest components, estimated Roblox risks, and manual setup notes.
Record concrete object, material, texture, and triangle counts for all Phase 1 export-candidate geometry.
Define an export-only collection or equivalent export group for WholeCell_ContextSocket and Lysosome_RootRig support objects.
Before any Roblox-bound export, apply transforms to mesh export candidates or document why a mesh is helper-only and excluded from export.
Mark procedural node materials and displacement as bake-required, Roblox-simplified, or debug-only.
Keep the missing full lysosome components blocked until later phases create export-conscious geometry using the exact manifest names.
phase_scope_notes: Phase 1 intentionally creates only scene infrastructure, root rig, and whole-cell socket.
The current scaffold is useful for future animation and integration planning, but it does not satisfy the required Roblox export package criteria yet.
The phase should be revised or supplemented with export documentation before continuing to Phase 2 under the project workflow rules.
